description:
Goal
Make  the authoritative owner of compiler stage sequencing so frontend preparation, execution-graph preparation, native preflight, and engine handoff are driven from one place instead of being split across , , and .

Signal
-  exposes the production pipeline entrypoints used by CLI / REPL / tests.
-  no longer owns the top-level native preflight sequencing; it consumes prepared inputs from the compiler spine.
- The authoritative-plan contract remains intact: planning still happens before codegen, and codegen emits no new  events.
- Targeted pipeline tests and the fixture matrix pass.

Strategy
1. Introduce Compiler-owned driver APIs for frontend and execution-graph preparation, then move shared callers onto them.
2. Extract the native preflight/materialization sequence out of the codegen driver into a Compiler-owned stage boundary.
3. Finish by routing CLI/REPL/test-runner/interpreter entrypoints through the new spine and updating the agent docs.

Plan
title: Epic: factor compiler spine into Compiler
